free_text: Diffraction Scattering of alpha-Particles from Even
Isotopes of Ni at Backward Angles
External beam line of the CSIR variable
energy cyclotron.
The beam was stopped in a Faraday collector 250 cm
behind the target position and the beam current was
integrated by means of a precision integrator.
Si surface-barrier detector and conventional
electronics. A Si surface-barrier detector at a
scattering angle of 30 degr. served to monitor the
product of the beam intensity and target thickness.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
The experimental absolute differential
cross-section scales are only accurate to within 12%,
due to errors in the evaluation of target thickness,
experimental geometry and beam current integration.
All thicknesses were determined to an absolute
uncertainty of less than 8 % by weighing the targets
on a microbalance.
SD
Target was self-supporting 58Ni target of 1.1 mg/cm2,
enriched to an isotopic purity of more than 98 %.
The statistical uncertainty is typically less
than 3 % and always less than 10 %, except for those
cases indicated by an appropriate error bar in the
figures giving the experimental results.
